posts_found.author: Donald Trump
posts_found.content: Any country found to be supplying military weapons to Iran will immediately face tariffs of 50% on any and all goods imported to the United States, effective immediately. There will be no exclusions or exemptions.

posts_found.author: Michael Burry
posts_found.role: Scion Asset Management, famous short seller
posts_found.content: Anthropic is eating Palantir's lunch. Anthropic captured 73% of all new enterprise spending. Went from $9B to $30B ARR in months. It took $PLTR 20 years to get to $5 Billion. PLTR can have government, which is low margin and small. Palantir's FDE model charges for human labor — Anthropic offers an API. $PLTR is the next Yahoo. [Post has since been deleted but caused ~$23B in PLTR market cap loss]

posts_found.author: Ray Dalio
posts_found.content: The Big Thing: We Are In A World War That Isn't Going To End Anytime Soon. The US-Israel-Iran war is just part of a world war that we are in. We are at Step 9 of a 13-step cycle. Global markets are failing to adequately price in the long-term implications of a fragmented, multipolar conflict environment — leaving portfolios exposed to sustained inflationary pressures, regime shifts in global trade, and heightened volatility across asset classes. The post-1945 world order is dead.

posts_found.author: Michael Saylor
posts_found.content: Bitcoin has likely bottomed near $60K. Forced sellers have been exhausted. The quantum threat to Bitcoin is theoretical and likely decades away — even then it's solvable. Bitcoin's open-source structure will allow developers to introduce quantum-resistant upgrades well before attacks become practical. BTC's next bull run will be driven by banking and digital credit adoption.

critical_alerts: Donald Trump (TRUTH): 50% tariffs on ALL goods from any country supplying military weapons to Iran — effective immediately, no exclusions. Score: 82.
Michael Burry (@michaeljburry): Deleted post called $PLTR 'the next Yahoo' as Anthropic captures 73% of new enterprise spend — PLTR closed -7.3%, shedding $23B in market cap. Score: 80.
summary: CRITICAL: Trump's immediate 50% Iran-weapons tariff threat (global trade escalation) and Burry's deleted PLTR short call (-$23B market cap) dominate; Dalio escalates world war warning as Iran ceasefire negotiations remain fragile.
